title: Epigeneium treacherianum
intro: Epigeneium treacherianum is a terrestrial orchid species, commonly known as the "tall epigeneium", which belongs to the orchid family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cold, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Epigeneium treacherianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Epigeneium treacherianum include decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are often used to treat respiratory disorders, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Epigeneium treacherianum is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a monopodial epiphyte native to the Himalayas. This plant is classified as a genus Epigeneium, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. Epigeneium treacherianum is often placed in the subtribe Gastrodieae, part of the tribe Epidendroideae. The classification of Epigeneium treacherianum reflects its unique characteristics within the orchid family.
